tribe_v2_insight: Superlative and hyperbolic language produces elevated semantic activation in temporal language areas, priming emotional rather than analytical processing
user_explanation: This uses loaded language — emotionally charged words designed to impress rather than inform. Superlatives and hyperbole prime your brain's language areas for emotional processing, making claims feel more significant than they may actually be.
legal_note: Unsubstantiated superlative claims in advertising may violate FTC truth-in-advertising standards. Words like 'guaranteed' and 'proven' require substantiation.
